title: Fix Your Wi-Fi With One of These Tempting Router Deals
gist: Wired's Prime Day 2026 router roundup signals Wi-Fi 7 mesh as the dominant upgrade category, with Netgear Orbi and Amazon's Eero positioned as premium picks while TP-Link competes on price despite an unresolved FCC foreign-made router ban that could constrain its future US business.

themes.theme: Wi-Fi 7 mesh as upgrade cycle driver

insights.claim: Tri-band Wi-Fi 7 mesh systems (2.4, 5, and 6 GHz) are now the editorially recommended default upgrade for most home networking buyers, displacing single routers and older Wi-Fi 6/6E gear.
insights.speaker: Simon Hill
insights.so_what: Watch for an accelerating consumer networking refresh cycle benefiting Wi-Fi 7 chipset and router makers as 6E/6 gear gets discounted to clear.

themes.theme: FCC foreign-made router ban risk for TP-Link

insights.claim: TP-Link, which manufactures overseas, has not yet secured an exemption from the FCC's foreign-made router ban, though the rule applies only to new systems and not existing inventory.
insights.speaker: Simon Hill
insights.so_what: Regulatory overhang could disrupt TP-Link's US market access; competitors like Netgear and Amazon Eero stand to gain share if the ban tightens.
insights.quote: Although TP-Link, which manufactures overseas, has not yet secured an exemption from the FCC's foreign-made router ban, that only applies to new systems.
insights.locator: The Deco BE63 is a powerful tri-band

insights.claim: The Netgear Orbi 770 2-pack hit $425 from $550 (23% off), described as the best price ever seen for the system, indicating premium mesh inventory is being discounted aggressively to move during Prime Day.
insights.speaker: Simon Hill
insights.so_what: Deep discounting on flagship mesh suggests soft demand or channel inventory pressure at the high end of home networking.
insights.quote: This is the best price I've ever seen for this system.

insights.claim: TP-Link's Deco BE63 delivered the steepest discounts in the roundup at up to 29% off, while the Asus RT-BE58 Go travel router was cut 41% to $95, showing budget and travel categories carry the deepest markdowns.
insights.speaker: Simon Hill
insights.so_what: Value-tier and niche router segments compete on price aggressively, pressuring margins for lower-end networking SKUs.

insights.claim: Amazon's Eero locks features like parental controls, advanced security, and ad blocking behind an Eero Plus subscription costing $10 per month or $100 per year, extending recurring revenue from networking hardware.
insights.speaker: Simon Hill
insights.so_what: Hardware-plus-subscription model signals router makers chasing recurring revenue; an investor angle for Amazon's services attach rate.
insights.locator: But you need an Eero Plus subscription
